summary: The user is planning a hike in California and seeks recommendations for easy trails suitable for beginners. They decide on the Mirror Lake Trail in Yosemite National Park and inquire about the best way to get to Yosemite from Los Angeles, ultimately choosing to drive and considering a stop at Tunnel View for scenic views.
facts: The user is planning a hike in California and is looking for easy trails suitable for beginners. The user has chosen the Mirror Lake Trail in Yosemite National Park for their hike. The Mirror Lake Trail is a 4-mile out-and-back trail with minimal elevation gain of about 100 feet. The user plans to drive approximately 310 miles from Los Angeles to Yosemite National Park, which takes about 4-5 hours. The user is considering stopping at Tunnel View on the way to Yosemite Valley for scenic views. The user has heard that the scenic route from LA to Yosemite is beautiful.
